item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely love this one—it’s an entity with nearly unlimited power but we can contain it by watching cheesy horror movies with it. The note at the end is a little bit chilling.
I love articles that make the Foundation think of creative containment procedures. This one is one of those wonderful pieces.

item.title: Classification
item.content:encoded:
The most solid reason I have seen for why this could be classified as Keter instead of Euclid is due to how the difficulty in containment increases exponentially with only a single containment breach. He gets out one time, and finds out real scary things = suddenly, the old containment procedures don't work anymore, and we have no way to hold him reliably.

It's true that his current containment is relatively easy, and seemingly not on Keter level. However, given the problems with re-containment in the event of a breach, I think that justifies it.

The note at the end of the article… implies this, but it seems to focus too much on the danger of such a situation, which has nothing to do with SCP Classification.

item.title: Re: Euclid
item.content:encoded:
Idk. This SCP doesn't have ridiculosly complex containment procedures, but I feel the Keter classification is warranted because its containment procedures, while simple, are incredibly easy to mess up due to how hard it is to take seriously. I can imagine it's very easy for Foundation personnel to fail to keep a straight face around it, and if that happens enough times it's only a matter of time before it finds out the foundation is lying to it.
Also, I think wether something is euclid or keter should factor in not only how hard it is to currently keep contained but also how hard it could be to re-contain in case of a breach.
Like… 173 and 096 have containment procedures that are just as easy (if not easier) to involuntarily mess up as this, but re-containing them would be basically trivial compared to how hard recontaining this thing could potentially be once it learns the truth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If he is so friendly to people after the fact, how would he react if he caused harm through his scare tactics? Would he feel remorse for his acrions? He seems like a prankster more or less, and typically if pranksters cause harm, they tend to be remorseful over it. Would he? And if he ever did would he stop doing it or just tone it back?

item.title: Re: Euclid
item.content:encoded: Otherwise a good point, but 096 is Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like if SCP-2006 got out he would become like the likes of Pennywise from It.

item.title: Re: Euclid
item.content:encoded:
Now couple that with an entity that possesses shape-shifting abilities with no known limits, and you'll understand why it's classified as Keter.

The same note does a pretty good job explaining why it's a keter. Take SCP-096 for example, it's classified as keter and falls under the "very difficult or costly to contain" spectrum. This guy is the same idea. He has potentially unlimited power and if he learned how to use it correctly, it wouldn't be difficult for him to escape containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This dude's surprisingly wholesome for a shape shifting potentially world ending anomaly. I can only imagine that if he gets out and actually destroys the word, in the wake of his destruction he goes "Ha, got you pretty good guys" above the sound of screaming.

+1

item.title: Euclid
item.content:encoded:
From the guide:

A Keter SCP does not mean the SCP is dangerous, just that it is simply very difficult or costly to contain

This should be Euclid IMO

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Besides, there's a whole Addendum dedicated to explaining that very thing. Just saying.

(btw I meant to reply to Milotic)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First off: you should edit your post instead of replying per the Site Rules.

Secondly: greenlights are only used in the Ideas Critique Forum to allow new authors to post a link to their draft in the Drafts Critique Forum; and not for highly successful articles which are already on the main site.

Thirdly: as someone who hasn't written 3 successful main site articles, isn't a member of staff or the butterfly squad, and who has not been featured in the Reviewers Spotlight, you can't give out greenlights.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is that a thing here or did I say it wrong ( talking about greenlit)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: greenlit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 2006 being a living thinking being and that plus the absolute risk that it holds can chock it up to beings Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with many of the people here that the addendum really makes this one special. Before reading the addendum, you wonder why it's not labeled -J, but it's a great statement about why the Foundation exists.
My only question is: Why is it labeled Keter? It poses a great threat if it were to escape, but there isn't much explanation as to why it is difficult to contain. I think adding just a little bit on that would clear things up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This should be brought down to Euclid because while the SCP is sentient and does try to scare people, it doesn't attempt to escape containment in any way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Adorable. I usually want to be creeped out by SCP`s, but this one is great for a subtle comic relief. The note does add a hint of menace though! This SCP would be a deadly ally to any number of apocalyptic SCP`s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's part of what endears it to me. Like, the fear that it could escape and become a world-ending abomination is founded since it is indestructible, but in- and out-of-universe, I feel like everybody is jumping to that conclusion without a whole lot of evidence. This guy reminds me of no one so much as Jack Skellington, and I think that if he were to ever escape, it would be more like an episode of Scooby-Doo than a breach of 682's magnitude.

I’m having trouble thinking what it might have been doing before

Given how bad it is at scaring people now, and how it thinks it has improved by watching garbage like Robot Monster, I imagine it's previous methods were floating up to people as a sphere and asking "Are you scared yet?" over and over until they left.
The great Ro-Man has conquered your "upvote" system, and has procured a thousand of them! Tremble in fear of his cumulative internet points!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's too late
We've got to operate
Just try to relax
It's a house of wax!

Oh, I remember Frankenstein
Shivers up my spine, whoa-oh ~
I'm for getting out of here
No need to shout, my dear, no-oh ~

It's like a movie --
It's a B-movie show!
It's like a movie --
It's a B-movie show!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hope it never changes it’s current form. I don’t care how ridiculous it looks, I’v always loved Ro-Man’s look.

item.title: Re: I give this picture to the writer of 2006. With my respect
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic, and I'm adding it to my author page this moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this could be more effective if there were some kind of indication that 2006 is capable of escalating in the manner site management is afraid of, but I’m not really sure how to do that. The only thing that comes to mind is if we knew that the SCP had already been improving itself in the wild until the Foundation managed to stall it, but I’m having trouble thinking what it might have been doing before that would fit as superficially-less-scary-but-still-trying-to-be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can we give Doctor Louef an Oscar please and thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really have anything constructive to contribute but I would like to make it very clear that I absolutely would die for this creature.

item.title: I give this picture to the writer of 2006. With my respect
item.content:encoded:
+ SCP-2006
I WILL EAT YOUR SOOOOUL!

Let me out of here! I'm going to die of fright!

It takes about a 1 minute to draw

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if it realizes that true fear is the consequent harm/death creatures cause?

item.title: Re: Keter?
item.content:encoded: I find it odd to how you don't see why it's classified as Keter when that is the whole point of the ending note that explains it.

item.title: Re: Keter?
item.content:encoded:
173 couldn't be keter if it escapes, and in fact, it would be much easier to contain if it escaped because there would be so many more people looking at it.

2006 has the ability to transform into what it believes is scary for us. Which means that one of its staff made an offhand mention about how he was afraid of catching the black plague, 2006 could suddenly turn into the actual black plague. Or he could turn into the concept of debt, or war, or death itself. How do you stop death?

item.title: Keter?
item.content:encoded:
I must admit - this is awesome! The idea, the execution… but why Keter? It could potentially break containment I guess… but why Keter if it hasn't proven to be a threat? If Keter is due to some hypothetical that likely won't happen given its current state, why make it Keter? That's like saying that 'whah, 173 MUST be keter because what if it escapes?' or 'The staircase must be Keter, because what if the face inside escapes?' I think it should be classed as safe - current protocol seems to be 100% effective, and so there's no risk or even attempt at escape.

I'd love to be proven wrong, but I honestly am baffled by the classification

Still, happy to give you a +1. Keep up the good skips!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this SCP. The potential it has for potential harm, and its jovial and excited manner in scaring people is great. A very well written SCP, it's so easy for it to become much more dangerous, if it's not in the Foundation's care.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry, and you trying to imply that anything, even the end of the universe itself, can even slightly faze the all-mighty Ro-man? You disgust me, mortal.

/s

In all seriousness, if you managed to scare this thing, it'd probably take notes and learn to be more scary from that experience.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just curious. What would happen if you scare it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
its the ending note that really sells this one, otherwise it would just seem like a J class.

nice feint to lead up to that sucker punch

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hell yes, 1370 and 2006 in the same room. Amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hah. A shapeshifter that just wants to scare people because it's fun. No evil intentions to date, just intention to strike fear into people.

Is there any "TRUE form?"

For now no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He might cause major havoc if he broke containment, but I can't help but feel bad, and I don't know why…
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I owe everyone an apology for not having known about this or upvoted it sooner

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It wants to be scary? Mission bloody well accomplished. You realize what it's capable of, what it wants, and how the only thing keeping it in check is the ability of site staff to keep a straight face around it? It is already fridge horror personified.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
He's so spoopy!!!

https://youtu.be/IMjZZWMNF6A

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is terrifying. May the box remain closed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We have nothing to fear but fear itself…
…shit.

In all honesty, this is probably my favourite keter scip of them all. It shows why what the foundation does is so important, and that they really are making a difference, something that it's easy to forget with a lot of safe/euclid scips.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"imagine if it saw the horror and fear of war, or the concepts of paranoia or phobias common to each and every human being."

Every totalitarian regime or any fear-mongering tactic contains the possibility of having been the result of an unknown instance of another SCP-2006. (E.g. Hitler and Stalin could have both been versions of SCP-2006, with Hitler invoking fear directed at a group, and Stalin creating fear of himself and his laws.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And to think it was based of a shit movie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Wikipedia article for Robot Monster contains a mention of SCP-2006. How cool is that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its Keter because if it were to escape and learn what truly scared humans. Not Ro-man, stuff like disease, or nuclear war, or the Holocaust. And since it shape-shifting abilities are endless…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I opened this SCP thinking it was going to be funny. Now, I only have one word to describe this SCP…

shit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user is permanently banned for trolling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a "pandora's box" type of keter: mostly harmless as long as the lid stays on.

But imagine if it discovered the slenderman mythos. Or saw a Kaiju flick. Or learned about the Black Plague.

And then note this line:

Currently, there is no known way to damage SCP-2006.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this scp, and how it's original. though I don't really understand the keter level, due to how easy it is to contain it.

item.title: +6spooky
item.content:encoded: Congrats you silly robot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent execution and concept! Take my upvote, if only I could give more than one.

item.title: Wonderful
item.content:encoded: I love this Scp, its a funny concept, an this is the first Keter class Scp I've read that doesn't want to really hurt anyone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
haha when i saw the "must believe these have a superb grasp at horror" i was like what? why? wait… oh dear god

+1 incredibly original

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. Short sweet and to the point. There is one thing though that makes it so that it doesn't get my vote. Where was the SCP found? What alerted the foundation to its presence. If this was explained you would get my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This made me think of an old story by Robert Bloch, "Talent" (1960), which explores a similar situation, but with a much, much darker outcome. I highly recommend.

N.B.
– hide block

I am not alleging plagiarism or anything like that. I just thought "Talent" was an interesting parallel. Incidentally, "Talent" leaves no doubt why it would be Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was also confused why it was keter til the last note, followed by a face palm. Up voted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dr. Louef deserves Academy Acting award in addition to his effort in trying to contain this stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's interesting to think about what it would do if it broke containment. Would it cause serious havoc? Would it look at war, phobias, pandemics, etc. and go "whoah… that's too hard core"
It's personality seems playful and fun, but 1048 seemed pretty safe at first too.

There's a lot of room for imagination. Upvoted.
